yesterdayStoryline.headline: Morocco beat hosts; France win a scrappy tie
yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: Ounahi brace downs Canada; Mbappé penalty settles an ill‑tempered France–Paraguay match

todayStoryline.headline: Brazil and England back in action
todayStoryline.subtitle: Brazil meet Norway while Mexico host England in two late fixtures

yesterday.scorers: Azzedine Ounahi 50', Azzedine Ounahi 82', Soufiane Rahimi 90+8' (MAR)

lines: Morocco took control after halftime with clinical counters and set-piece quality.
The win knocks hosts Canada out and sends Morocco into the last eight.
France struggled to break Paraguay's low block until a late penalty.
Mbappé's spot-kick settles the tie and sets up a Morocco quarter-final clash.

facts.home: Brazil adopted its iconic yellow-green kit after a 1953 national design competition.
facts.away: Norway's population is about 5.5 million, relatively small for a top-tier European football nation.

facts.home: Jorge Campos famously designed and wore flamboyant multicoloured goalkeeper shirts for Mexico in the 1990s.
facts.away: England's 1966 World Cup trophy was famously recovered by a dog called Pickles after theft.

news.headline: Trump delivers July 4 address on National Mall after storm delay
news.body: President Donald Trump delivered a July 4 address on the National Mall after a storm-related evacuation delayed the event. Authorities earlier cleared and sheltered crowds for safety, and the ceremony drew scrutiny over security and crowd management.

footerNote: All times converted from official kickoffs
